commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The branch is review-ready with no narrow automated repair identified; the remaining action is maintainer API/session-policy acceptance and normal check gating.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff does not touch dependencies, workflows, lockfiles, secrets, or artifact execution paths. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional `chat.send.resumeSession` reconnect hint through Control UI, Gateway protocol and Swift generation, session initialization, docs, and regression tests so a visible webchat session can be preserved after reconnect.

PR surface: Source +53, Tests +168, Docs +1, Other +4. Total +226 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and the linked report, but I did not live-reproduce the Mac sleep/Docker/Chrome path here. Current main sends the visible `sessionId` without a reconnect hint and `initSessionState` rotates stale entries when freshness fails.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The branch is review-ready with no narrow automated repair identified; the remaining action is maintainer API/session-policy acceptance and normal check gating.

target.title: fix #87700: [Bug]: Control UI webchat session silently resets after network disconnect / sleep — previous session file renamed to .jsonl.reset.* and lost from sessions tab
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI can keep showing a conversation after gateway reconnect, but the next send can roll the stale transcript into `.jsonl.reset.*` and make it disappear from the sessions tab.
- Solution: pass the client-selected `sessionId` through `chat.send`, but only allow it to suppress idle/daily rollover when Control UI also sends the one-shot reconnect resume signal.
- What changed: `chat.send` now accepts `resumeSession`, Control UI sets it after a gateway disconnect for the current visible session, and `initSessionState` preserves configured idle/daily rollover for ordinary sends.
- Review repairs: the Swift protocol generator defaults optional `ChatSendParams.resumeSession` to `nil`, the generated Swift model is refreshed, and the gateway protocol/session docs document the one-shot reconnect exception.
- Sync note: this branch was rebased onto current `origin/main` after the drift gate reported related upstream changes and conflicts.
- What did NOT change: explicit `/new`, mismatched session ids, and ordinary stale Control UI sends without the reconnect resume signal still rotate normally.
- Fixes #87700

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** after a sleep/disconnect cycle, Control UI can still hold the visible session id. A reconnect resume send should keep that session instead of rolling the transcript to `.reset.*`, while ordinary stale sends still follow the configured reset policy.
- **Real environment tested:** live local OpenClaw dev gateway started from the rebased PR worktree on Linux at head `699c78031d8637251e65d8c6c52ea70fd49cb1a2`, using an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`/state dir and a real WebSocket `connect` + `chat.send` request. The proof pre-seeded a stale `sessions.json` entry and transcript file, paired a temporary signed webchat device, sent `chat.send` with `sessionId` and `resumeSession: true` through the running gateway, then inspected the actual session store and sessions directory.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```sh
  OPENCLAW_REPO="$TASK_WORKTREE" OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19130 start_openclaw_dev_gateway.sh
  cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19130 node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/live-gateway-session-proof.mjs"

  pnpm protocol:gen
  pnpm protocol:gen:swift
  OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts \
    src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts
  pnpm tsgo:test
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** terminal console output from the fresh post-rebase live gateway proof:
  ```text
  gatewayConnect= ok
  chatSend= ok
  resumeSession= true
  storeSessionBefore= sess-live-reconnect-proof
  storeSessionAfter= sess-live-reconnect-proof
  resetFiles= 0
  proof= live gateway chat.send resumeSession preserved the asserted webchat session without .reset rollover
  ```
  Focused verification after the rebase also passed:
  ```text
  [test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 187.82s
  pnpm tsgo:test passed
  git diff --check passed
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** the running gateway accepted the real webchat `chat.send`, kept the `agent:main:main` store entry on the original session id, and produced zero `.reset.*` transcript files. Focused regression tests also prove the same asserted `sessionId` does not bypass idle/daily rollover unless the reconnect resume signal is present.
- **What was not tested:** I did not reproduce a physical Mac sleep/Docker/Chrome wake cycle, and this environment cannot run the Mantis/Desktop visual proof. The live proof covers the same gateway/WebSocket send path and session-store rollover decision in a local isolated gateway process; the Control UI unit tests cover the one-shot reconnect marker.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts`, `packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts`, `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/logs-chat.ts`, `scripts/protocol-gen-swift.ts`, `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift`, and `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`.
- **Scenario locked in:** gateway `chat.send` forwards `sessionId` and `resumeSession`, Control UI emits `resumeSession` once after reconnect, reconnect resume preserves the visible session, ordinary stale sends with only `sessionId` still rotate, the Swift generated initializer remains source-compatible for callers that omit the optional flag, and public docs describe the reset-policy exception.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the issue is caused by the handoff between Control UI reconnect state, the gateway `chat.send` request, and the session reset owner, so tests cover each boundary plus the stale-rollover compatibility case; the generator/docs repair keeps the public protocol contract source-compatible and documented.
- **Commands run after rebasing onto current main:**
  ```sh
  pnpm protocol:gen
  pnpm protocol:gen:swift
  OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts \
    src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts
  pnpm tsgo:test
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Test output:** protocol generation completed without producing dirty generated diffs; the focused Vitest command passed 5 shards in 187.82s; `pnpm tsgo:test` passed; `git diff --check` passed.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `chat.send` parsed the client-supplied `sessionId`, but reply initialization did not receive a reconnect-specific resume intent. Passing `sessionId` alone was not enough because Control UI also sends the current session id on ordinary follow-up sends, so treating any matching id as fresh would disable configured idle/daily rollover.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** Control UI now records the visible session id when the gateway disconnects and sends `resumeSession` once when the next send targets that same session. `initSessionState` only treats the matching requested id as reusable when that reconnect resume signal is present; otherwise the existing reset freshness policy remains the source of truth.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** The mock `return undefined` path in the gateway test only exposes captured reply options without running a model call. The `as unknown` casts are existing UI test/helper boundary patterns for exercising controller functions with partial host objects; they do not suppress type errors or change runtime behavior. The live gateway proof exercises the running WebSocket/RPC path and session store, and the session regression tests cover the ordinary stale rollover compatibility case.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The session store and existing reset policy remain the source of truth. This patch adds one additive `chat.send` protocol hint and does not add a new session store, config surface, or reset policy bypass for ordinary sends. The generator/docs repair keeps the public protocol surface source-compatible and documented.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — maintainer-decision:** the PR body now spells out the public `chat.send.resumeSession` contract and reset-policy exception. The remaining decision is whether maintainers accept that additive contract.
- **RF-002 — fixed:** the branch was rebased onto current `origin/main` (`f60943717ef57412872233a45f2b61e4f95f4c2a`), the related conflicts were resolved, and post-rebase local verification passed. GitHub mergeability still needs to be rechecked after this branch is pushed.
- **RF-003 — maintainer-decision:** the new wire parameter is optional and documented, with protocol schema, generated Swift model, docs, and regression tests aligned. Maintainers still own the final API-surface acceptance.
- **RF-004 — addressed with bounded risk:** the one matching Control UI reconnect send can preserve a session that idle/daily freshness would otherwise rotate, but ordinary stale sends without `resumeSession` still rotate and stale/mismatched ids are not resumed.
- **RF-005 — addressed locally / maintainer-decision remains:** conflict resolution and proof updates are complete locally; the remaining judgment is the maintainer/API-policy decision on the documented reconnect contract.

## Merge risk

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** reconnecting Control UI sends can preserve the visible session instead of losing the transcript to `.reset.*` rollover.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** without this, a user can return from sleep or a network disconnect, send into the visible conversation, and see that conversation disappear from the sessions tab.
- **Risk labels considered:** public protocol/API surface and session-state behavior.
- **Risk explanation:** `resumeSession` is a new optional `chat.send` wire parameter, and a matching Control UI reconnect send intentionally creates one narrow exception to daily/idle stale rollover.
- **Why acceptable:** the field is additive, the exception remains owned by `initSessionState`, ordinary stale sends still rotate, stale/mismatched ids are rejected by the existing lifecycle owner, and the contract is covered by schema/docs/tests plus a live Gateway/WebSocket proof.

## `chat.send.resumeSession` Contract

This change adds `resumeSession?: boolean` to `chat.send` as a one-shot reconnect hint. It is not a general stale-session override.

The gateway only considers the hint when all of these are true:

- the client also sends a non-empty `sessionId`;
- that `sessionId` still matches the gateway's current stored `entry.sessionId` for the resolved `sessionKey`;
- the existing session entry is otherwise reusable;
- this is the next send after the Control UI observed a reconnect condition.

If `resumeSession` is absent, ordinary stale sends keep the existing behavior: daily/idle freshness still rotates to a new `sessionId`. If `resumeSession` is true but the requested `sessionId` is stale, mismatched, or belongs to another current session, the hint is ignored and the normal reset policy applies.

In short: `sessionId` asserts which current transcript the client believes it is viewing; `resumeSession` says the assertion came from a reconnect resume attempt. The session lifecycle owner still makes the final reuse/rollover decision.

## Risk Boundaries

This PR intentionally keeps the reset-policy exception narrow:

- It does not make `sessionId` a general stale-session bypass.
- It does not change ordinary daily reset behavior.
- It does not change ordinary idle reset behavior.
- It does not treat `updatedAt` as freshness authority.
- It does not make heartbeat, cron, exec, or gateway bookkeeping events extend session freshness.
- It does not resume a stale or mismatched `sessionId`.
- It does not move session lifecycle policy into the Control UI.
- It does not move session lifecycle policy into the Gateway method handler.
- It does not change channel-originated delivery semantics.

The remaining compatibility risk is that `resumeSession` is a new optional public `chat.send` wire parameter. The remaining session-state risk is that a matching Control UI reconnect send may preserve a session that daily/idle freshness would otherwise rotate. Both risks are explicit and covered by schema/docs/tests; the merge decision is whether maintainers accept that public contract and narrow policy exception.

### Additional targeted proof

The post-rebase focused proof covers the same contract boundaries:

- `resumeSession` is accepted by the Gateway protocol schema.
- Control UI records the current visible `sessionId` on WebSocket close.
- Control UI sends `resumeSession: true` only when the reconnect marker still matches the current visible `sessionId`, then clears the marker.
- A real Gateway server/WebSocket harness forwards `sessionId` + `resumeSession` through `chat.send` into reply initialization.
- The session lifecycle owner preserves only the matching current session for the reconnect resume path.
- Ordinary stale sends without `resumeSession` still rotate, and stale/mismatched requested session ids are not resumed.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
